steps.title: Identify what causes meander formation
steps.content: The notes state: “A meander forms when water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits that sediment on the opposite side.” This directly identifies the mechanism—erosion and redeposition of sediment—that causes meanders to form.

steps.title: Apply the mechanism to the choices
steps.content: Choice A identifies the cause: erosion and redeposition of sediment from one bank to the other forms meanders. The other three don't answer the question—they discuss sinuosity, meander behavior, and meander existence rather than what creates meanders in the first place. That matches choice A — A is correct.
